CLAUDE.md and Rules for AI: Writing Tips That Make AI Understand Your Project

Getting AI to actually fit into your dev workflow comes down to one thing — how well you write the rules file. Here are 8 writing tips for CLAUDE.md and Rules for AI that make AI understand what your project actually needs.
No crypto Package, No Node.js — The Web Crypto API Does It All in the Browser

Sooner or later you'll need to hash, encrypt, or decrypt data in the browser. In the past that meant Node.js's crypto module or a third-party package like crypto-js. But modern browsers already ship the Web Crypto API, so you can do all of it with zero dependencies. This post walks through hashing, AES encryption/decryption, HMAC signing, RSA, and a few handy helpers.
Not Sure How to Optimize Claude Code? One Simple Command Points You in the Right Direction!

Been using Claude Code for a while and don't know how to optimize it? With just one /insights command, you can analyze your usage from the past 28 days and get concrete suggestions on what to improve.
Gemini Gems Tutorial: Easily Build Your Own AI Assistant

As AI gets used everywhere, more people are trying to build their own AI assistants to help with all kinds of tasks. This post walks through Gemini Gems — Google's service that lets you easily build your own AI assistant and use it across various platforms.
A Safer Alternative to git push --force: git push --force-with-lease

After using git rebase or git commit --amend to rewrite history, you have to force-push to update the remote. But git push --force overwrites the remote unconditionally — which can blow away your teammates' work. This post introduces a safer alternative: git push --force-with-lease and --force-if-includes, which add a layer of protection before you force-push.
macOS Time Machine Taking Forever? One Trick to Speed It Up

macOS Time Machine is a great backup tool, but the backup process sometimes gets painfully slow — especially if you haven't run it for a while. This post walks through the fastest way to get Time Machine going again, so backing up stops being a chore.
Want to Develop With Claude Code? This Guide Takes You From Beginner to Advanced

As a developer, you've probably heard of AI Agent dev tools like Gemini, Codex, Claude Code, and so on. Most developers these days subscribe to a few AI Agent tools to speed up their dev workflow. This post picks one AI Agent tool — Claude Code — and walks through how to use it for development. There may be acceleration techniques and usage concepts here you don't know yet, and this post includes practical examples so you can get up to speed faster.
Git init Shows a master Branch Hint? Here's How to Set main as Default

Some of my students were running into a hint that says "master" is set as the initial branch name when they used Git, and they were curious about what was going on. So I wrote a quick post to log this down.
Use Dev Containers to Build a Safe, Isolated Environment for AI-Driven Development

A lot of people are excited about automated development — some would love to let AI Agents handle everything end-to-end. But there are real concerns: security, environment consistency, dependency management, and so on. There have already been multiple incidents where Auto AI Agents, with no isolated environment, ran commands directly on the host and wiped systems or destroyed data. So this post is about how to use Dev Containers to build an isolated development environment where AI Agents can work in a controlled space.
What Is Git Worktree? The Secret to Running Multiple AI Agents in One Project

Most developers are using AI Agents to help build things now, but real projects usually have multiple features in flight at once. So the question becomes: can one project only run one AI Agent at a time? This post walks through how Git Worktree lets you run several AI Agents in the same project — at the same time.
